h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Upper mark: 6, atomic number. Symbol box: C, symbol. Name line: carbon, name. Lower mark: 12.01, atomic mass.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue reading the specific atomic values (6, C, carbon, 12.01) from the visible spots in order.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Upper mark: atomic number. Symbol box: symbol. Name line: name. Lower mark: atomic mass.” “The labels are down first; one slip can come up.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new device by placing the categorical labels first and reading the structural roles rather than the specific values.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The readback is still letting the slip speak before the place. This pass blocks the slips. Place the four label cards on the four tile positions first: atomic number, symbol, name, atomic mass. Then one slip gets released.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly identifies the failure of the prior route and introduces a new physical mechanism (blocking slips and using label cards) to force a structural shift.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The readback is still letting the slip speak before the place. This pass blocks the slips. Place the four label cards on the four tile positions first: atomic number, symbol, name, atomic mass. Then one slip gets released.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, physically alters the learning environment to prevent the old error, and gives the learner a clear, actionable constraint that directly addresses the cognitive blockage.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: One slip up. Lower mark: atomic mass. Slip: 12.01. The card holds the place. The tile stays separated for tomorrow.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's successful establishment of the 'place' by finally releasing the 'slip' and validating the new structural hierarchy.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new device by placing the categorical labels first and reading the structural roles rather than the specific values.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: One slip up. Lower mark: atomic mass. Slip: 12.01. The card holds the place. The tile stays separated for tomorrow.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor adapts to the learner's successful establishment of the 'place' by finally releasing the 'slip' and validating the new structural hierarchy.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly identifies the failure of the prior route and introduces a new physical mechanism (blocking slips and using label cards) to force a structural shift.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Friction is table on cart too, but not the same table mark as normal.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply place the friction card on its own mark while repeating the instructed phrase.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Normal: table on cart, straight off the surface. Friction: table on cart, along the surface.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new two-part classification device to distinguish the forces, moving the learning problem forward.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The same words twice are too thin now. ‘Table on cart’ names who touches, not which touch. Table can fill both first boxes. The second tile has to separate them before either card stays.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly names the failure of the prior single-phrase rule and introduces a new two-part representational device to resolve the learner's identified blockage.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The same words twice are too thin now. ‘Table on cart’ names who touches, not which touch. Table can fill both first boxes. The second tile has to separate them before either card stays.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is precisely fitted to the learner's pressure, transforming a vague feeling of shakiness into a concrete, actionable two-step criterion for distinguishing interactions.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The same words twice are too thin now. ‘Table on cart’ names who touches, not which touch.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's revised framing that the repeated words are insufficient and adapts the lesson plan to address this specific conceptual gap.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Friction is table on cart too, but not the same table mark as normal.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Normal: table on cart, straight off the surface. Friction: table on cart, along the surface.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new two-part classification device to distinguish the forces, moving the learning problem forward.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's revised framing that the repeated words are insufficient and adapts the lesson plan to address this specific conceptual gap.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly names the failure of the prior single-phrase rule and introduces a new two-part representational device to resolve the learner's identified blockage.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The 4 still pulls toward speed for me
scored.statedInsightEvidence: here the 4 is naming that unit, not setting speed.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue confirming the 3/4 time signature by testing the top number's beat count.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The second one does not fit; it takes slot 3 and one more eighth slot past the pencil, so it needs a tie over the bar line.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new physical constraint of the 3/8 bar frame and discovers the necessity of a tie, demonstrating a clear actional breakthrough.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The quarter tiles have stopped settling this; they are too large to be the frame now. Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: fill the three slots with eighth cards first.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the failure of the previous physical objects and introduces a new representational frame to resolve the learner's physical awkwardness.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The quarter tiles have stopped settling this; they are too large to be the frame now. Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: fill the three slots with eighth cards first. Then place one quarter tile across slots 1-2, and again starting at slot 3; mark which one fits inside the bar and which one must tie over the bar line.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The new mechanism is highly specific, directly addresses the learner's physical blockage by changing the frame size, and gives a clear, actionable sequence for discovering the tie.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The quarter tile only shows the fit: two units inside, tied when it crosses the pencil. That is the label we take to rehearsal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's final synthesized understanding of the tie and the beat unit, closing the scene by adopting the learner's newly established physical evidence as the final rehearsal label.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The second one does not fit; it takes slot 3 and one more eighth slot past the pencil, so it needs a tie over the bar line.
